data.title: WaFd GAAP EPS of $0.71 beats by $0.14, revenue of $188.69M beats by $1.78M
data.description:
• WaFd press release (NASDAQ:WAFD): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.71 beats by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $188.69M (+6.0% Y/Y) beats by $1.78M.

data.title: Teva settles patent litigation with Amicus
data.description:
• Amicus Therapeutics (NASDAQ:FOLD) has agreed to settle patent litigation it initiated against Teva Pharmaceutical (NYSE:TEVA) over the Israeli drugmaker’s plans to seek approval for a generic version of its best-seller, Galafold.
  • In a statement on Thursday, Princeton, New Jersey-based Amicus (NASDAQ:FOLD) said that under the agreement, Teva (NYSE:TEVA) receives a license to market a generic version of Galafold after January 30, 2037. The agreement will end the Hatch-Waxman litigation between the companies subject to regulatory clearance.
  • The litigation was related to Teva’s (TEVA) plans to market a Galafold generic before the expiry of Amicus’ (FOLD) patents applicable to the drug, which is indicated in the U.S. for a rare genetic condition known as Fabry disease.
  • The licensing agreement is expected to be finalized following a review at the U.S. Federal Trade Commission and the Department of Justice.

data.title: Microsoft leads software pack as VARs expect uptick in IT spending: survey
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), along with several cybersecurity firms, could experience an uplift during the first half of 2025 as KeyBanc's third quarter 2024 survey of value-added resellers, or VARs, shows an increase in IT budget expectations.

"As for 2024 IT budgets, there was a slight uptick in growth expectations, but the bigger increase came in 2025 IT budget expectations, which this group now forecasts to accelerate to 4.4% compared to a 3.2% expectation 90 days ago," said KeyBanc analysts, led by Jackson Ader, in an extensive investor note.

The results prompted KeyBanc to boost several price targets. Microsoft was increased to $505 from $490, Palo Alto Networks (NASDAQ:PANW) was raised to $435 from $400 and CyberArk Software (NASDAQ:CYBR) was elevated to $340 from $310. All three stocks have Overweight ratings from KeyBanc.

"Today, 37% of VARs expect to see an IT budget recovery in the first half of 2025," Ader said. "That is an uptick from last quarter and, importantly, begins in just a couple of months, meaning the visibility is higher than it was previously."

The upcoming U.S. presidential election prompted 26% of survey respondents to delay finalizing their 2025 IT budgets until the dust settles in the ballot boxes.

Another 46% of respondents indicated artificial intelligence spending is affecting other areas of IT budgets. They said AI and generative AI is most likely to detract from the front office, back office and security spending.

Still, 2025 security budgets are expected to increase by 14.4% in 2025, which is positive for Palo Alto, CyberArk and SentinelOne (NYSE:S).

The VAR survey follows positive results from KeyBanc's small to medium business Q3 IT survey, published on October 1. SMB respondents also expect a 4.4% increase to IT spending in 2025 compared to a 1.9% increase in 2024.

data.title: Texas Capital Bancshares cut to Neutral at Piper on ROA target skepticism
data.description:
Texas Capital Bancshares (NASDAQ:TCBI) stock slid 2.7% in early Friday trading after it reported Q3 earnings, then Piper Sandler downgraded the bank to Neutral as analyst Stephen Scouten remains "modestly skeptical" about the company's 1.10% return on asset target in H2 2025.

"We need to see much more progress before we get on board with the ~1.10% ROA in 2H25 (now pushed back from FY25)," the analyst wrote in a note to clients.

TCBI has been seeking to strengthen and diversify its revenue stream by building up its higher-margin businesses, such as investment banking and treasury services. However, loan growth has been slower than expected, Scouten said.

Texas Capital's (TCBI) variable rate loan book will be a headwind, and much of the company's growth in Q3 was driven by a portfolio acquisition rather than organic growth.

"While TCBI has made significant and admirable progress in executing its strategic plan, the slower pace of new client acquisition and modestly asset-sensitive balance sheet (less than reported given mortgage finance) lead to our skepticism around the achievement of the 2025 targets," the analyst wrote.

With Texas Capital (TCBI) stock climbing 38% since early August, "execution risk seems to outweigh any low-hanging fruit left in the share price recovery," he noted.

data.title: Amer Sports to appeal Norwegian court decision on cross-country ski bindings
data.description:
Amer Sports (NYSE:AS) said on Friday it will appeal the decision by the Norwegian Court of Appeal that prevents the parent company of Salomon and Atomic skis from selling its Cross-Country Shift and Shift Race binding systems in the country.

“We are disappointed by the outcome and disagree with the decision, as it impacts athletes who have dedicated their lives to their sport and limits consumer choice. While we will comply with the court’s instructions, we are actively evaluating our options and will be appealing this decision,” the company said in a statement.

Last week, the Oslo District Court decided in favor of Norway’s Rottefella’s patent infringement lawsuit against Amer Sports (AS), ruling that Amer Sports’ adjustable cross country ski bindings infringed on the intellectual property of Rottefella’s MOVE binding system. MOVE allows skiers to effortlessly adjust their bindings for grip or glide without removing their skis, even while moving.

Amer Sports (AS) launched a similar system three years later, used on Salomon and Atomic skis, prompting a lawsuit by Rottefella. The decision by the Oslo court, later upheld by the Norwegian Court of Appeals, prevents Amer Sports from selling and marketing their adjustable bindings, and awarded Rottefella 19.2M Norwegian kroner in damages and legal expenses.

Amer Sports (AS) shares are up 2.2%, more than reversing Thursday’s 1.3% loss.

data.title: Bullfrog AI announces pricing of offering and private placement for proceeds of $3.13M
data.description:
• BullFrog AI Holdings (NASDAQ:BFRG) said on Friday it has entered into a definitive agreement for the purchase and sale of 1.6M shares of common stock and common warrants at a combined purchase price of $2.
  • The gross proceeds to from the offering are expected to be approximately $3.13 million.

data.title: TSM under probe by US over potential dealings with China's Huawei: report
data.description:
Update: The story was updated with TSM's statement to Seeking Alpha.

The U.S. Commerce Department is probing if Taiwan Semiconductor (NYSE:TSM) has been making AI or smartphone chips for Chinese company Huawei Technologies, and violating U.S. export rules, The Information reported.

"TSMC is a law-abiding company and we are committed to complying with all applicable rules and regulations, including applicable export controls. We maintain a robust and comprehensive export system for monitoring and ensuring compliance," Taiwan Semiconductor said in an emailed statement to Seeking Alpha.

In recent weeks, the U.S. department has contacted TSM to ask if it was involved in manufacturing either smartphone chips or AI chips for Huawei, the report added.

The department is also checking if Huawei bought chips from TSM indirectly using intermediary companies with a different name to place orders on its behalf, the report noted.

The government body is also evaluating whether TSM carried out the required know-your-customer due diligence while accepting orders, according to the report.

"If we have any reason to believe there are potential issues, we will take prompt action to ensure compliance, including conducting investigations and proactively communicating with relevant parties including customers and regulatory authorities as necessary, TSM said in the email to Seeking Alpha.

Huawei was once in competition with Apple (AAPL) and Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) to be the world's biggest handset maker until U.S. restrictions, starting in 2019, began to curb its access to chip manufacturing tools needed to produce its most advanced models.

The U.S. banned the Chinese tech giant from buying chips made by using American parts, due to national security concerns. The restrictions also curbs Huawei from using U.S. technology to make its own chips without a greenlight from the Commerce Department.

TSM — which produces chips for some of the world's largest tech companies, including Apple, Nvidia and AMD — had previously signed a deal with the U.S. government to receive up to $6.6B in grants and as much as $5B in loans to boost domestic manufacturing of advanced semiconductors, under the U.S. CHIPS Act funding.

If the Commerce Department finds that TSM violated export rules in potential dealings with Huawei, it could impose a fine or more severe actions, such as temporarily halting the company's access to U.S. tech. Last year, the department fined Seagate Technology $300M for selling hard disk drive technology to Huawei, the report noted.

The probe's focus, partly, is also checking if TSM was involved in making AI chips designed by Huawei, the report added.

Huawei's AI server chips have seen rising demand in China as an alternate to Nvidia-made chips, following export curbs by the U.S. on advanced chips by Nvidia and other American companies. In August, it was reported that Huawei was nearing to unveil a new chip to use for AI. Huawei had told potential clients that the new chip is comparable to Nvidia's H100, which was unveiled last year and is not directly available in China.

The U.S. department is also investigating whether TSM made smartphone chips for Huawei's phones. Prior to the U.S. ban, Huawei was a big customer of TSM, according to the report.

Last August, Huawei surprised many by quietly launching its new flagship smartphone, Mate 60 Pro. The chip inside the phone ignited concerns in the U.S. and raised questions about how it was possible, without the company being able to access critical technologies. In April, Huawei unveiled its Pura 70 series, which features the Kirin 9010 chip, a follow-up to the Kirin 9000s reportedly made by Semiconductor Manufacturing International (OTCQX:SIUIF) for the Mate 60 Pro.

Huawei and its chip making partner Semiconductor Manufacturing International, reportedly, have been struggling with the production of the chips used in Huawei's next flagship phone Mate 70, due to the continuing impact of the U.S. ban.

data.title: Aura stock rallies 16% on positive bladder cancer data for bel-sar
data.description:
• Aura Biosciences (NASDAQ:AURA) stock rallied 16% in early trading Friday after the company released positive Phase 1 data for its drug candidate bel-sar in the treatment of non-muscle invasive bladder cancer, or NMIBC.
  • The study showed a complete response in four out of five patients in a subset with low-grade disease. Only Grade 1 drug-related adverse events were reported, with less than 10% experiencing such events, according to a statement.

data.title: Lamb Weston as activist Jana Partners pushes for sale (update)
data.description:
Update 9:45am: Adds Jana, Continental Grain confirmation, updates shares.

Lamb Weston (NYSE:LW) jumped 7% after Jana Partners disclosed it has built a stake and wants the french-fry maker to explore a sale.

Jana disclosed a 5% stake in Lamb Weston (LW) and Continental Grain has a less than 1% stake and Jane said it intends to have discussions with the board and management. The activist has concerns about capital spending, share repurchase activist and compensation practices.

"The litany of self-inflicted missteps that have led to underperformance for shareholders," Jana wrote in a 13D filing disclosing the stake.

Jana may decide to nominate candidates for the board and is working with industry executives including former Lamb Weston (LW) executive chairman, Timothy McLevish, according to a WSJ report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

Jana believes that Lamb Weston (LW), which has attracted potential buyers over the years, could be a target for larger food companies, according to the WSJ report. Jana has teamed up with agricultural investment firm Continental Grain on its LW investment.

The Jana push comes after Lamb Weston (LW) shares sunk almost 30% on July 24 after the french-fry maker missed earnings expectations with its FQ4 report and warned of a tough path ahead. Shares of the $10 billion company have dropped 34% this year.

Earlier this month, Lamb Weston (LW) announced it's cutting 4% jobs globally, shuttering an older plant in Connell, Washington, and will temporarily curtail certain production lines in North America in a restructuring move to reduce operating expenses.

The WSJ news on Lamb Weston (LW) also comes as activist investors, including Jana, are set to present their ideas at the 13D Monitor Active/-Passive Investor Summit in New York City on Tuesday.

data.title: SANUWAVE announces 1-for-375 reverse stock split
data.description:
• SANUWAVE Health (OTCQB:SNWVD) announced that the company implemented a 1-for-375 reverse stock split of its outstanding common stock.
  • The company’s common stock will continue to trade under the symbol “SNWV” on the OTCQB and is expected to begin trading on a split-adjusted basis at the opening of the market on October 18.
  • Upon the effectiveness of the reverse stock split, all of the holders of the company’s outstanding convertible notes and associated warrants issued in August 2022, November 2022, May 2023, December 2023, January 2024 and June 2024 were converted into approximately 4 million shares of post-split common stock.
  • In addition, NH Expansion Credit Fund Holdings, the agent under the company’s Note and Warrant Purchase and Security Agreement, exercised all of its outstanding warrants for 146,302 post-split shares of common stock.
  • The company also has sold approximately 1.3 million shares in a PIPE offering priced at a post-split price of $8.25 per share.
  • Gross proceeds were about $10.3 million, and the company did not engage a bank or agent in connection with the offering.
  • The use of proceeds will include working capital and general corporate purposes and the repayment of certain indebtedness.
  • Source: Press Release
